title: elaeocarpus inopportunus
intro: Elaeocarpus inopportunus, also known as the Queensland blue quandong, is a species of flowering plant in the family Elaeocarpaceae. This plant is used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Elaeocarpus inopportunus include its ability to treat various ailments such as fever, cough, and digestive issues, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve immune function. Herbal preparations of Elaeocarpus inopportunus include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the plant's leaves or fruits in hot water or a solvent.
